plssem 0.1.1
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Categorical Models with plssem
    • Interaction Models with plssem
    • Linear Models with plssem
    • Multilevel Models with plssem
    • Ordinal Regression with plssem
    • Parallel Bootstrapping with plssem

Multilevel Models with plssem

Source: vignettes/multilevel-models.Rmd
multilevel-models.Rmd

This vignette shows examples of multilevel random slopes and intercept models, with both continuous and ordinal data.

Random Slopes Model 

slopes_model <- "
  X =~ x1 + x2 + x3
  Z =~ z1 + z2 + z3
  Y =~ y1 + y2 + y3
  W =~ w1 + w2 + w3
  Y ~ X + Z + (1 + X + Z | cluster)
  W ~ X + Z + (1 + X + Z | cluster)
"

Continuous Indicators 

fit_slopes_cont <- pls(
  slopes_model,
  data      = randomSlopes,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50
)
summary(fit_slopes_cont)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 3 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                   PLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          LINEAR
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                          5000
#>   Number of iterations                               3
#>   Number of latent variables                         4
#>   Number of observed variables                      18
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                   127.210
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                50
#>   SRMR                                           0.008
#>   RMSEA                                          0.018
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   x1                                             0.860
#>   x2                                             0.683
#>   x3                                             0.772
#>   z1                                             0.839
#>   z2                                             0.694
#>   z3                                             0.759
#>   y1                                             0.838
#>   y2                                             0.726
#>   y3                                             0.750
#>   w1                                             0.840
#>   w2                                             0.695
#>   w3                                             0.771
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   Y                                              0.533
#>   W                                              0.547
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X =~          
#>     x1              0.927      0.010   93.137    0.000
#>     x2              0.826      0.011   72.280    0.000
#>     x3              0.879      0.006  138.146    0.000
#>   Z =~          
#>     z1              0.916      0.011   85.124    0.000
#>     z2              0.833      0.011   74.759    0.000
#>     z3              0.871      0.009   93.373    0.000
#>   Y =~          
#>     y1              0.915      0.011   79.830    0.000
#>     y2              0.852      0.011   78.276    0.000
#>     y3              0.866      0.014   62.722    0.000
#>   W =~          
#>     w1              0.916      0.011   84.298    0.000
#>     w2              0.834      0.011   74.415    0.000
#>     w3              0.878      0.013   70.088    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   Y ~           
#>     X               0.293      0.020   14.495    0.000
#>     Z               0.444      0.035   12.859    0.000
#>   W ~           
#>     X               0.393      0.033   11.733    0.000
#>     Z               0.248      0.047    5.319    0.000
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .Y               0.009      0.005    1.999    0.046
#>    .W               0.009      0.008    1.260    0.208
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X ~~          
#>     Z               0.175      0.010   17.348    0.000
#>   Y~X ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.003      0.006   -0.588    0.557
#>   Y~Z ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.024      0.012   -2.041    0.041
#>     Y~X             0.012      0.008    1.495    0.135
#>   W~X ~~        
#>     W~1             0.003      0.009    0.329    0.742
#>   W~Z ~~        
#>     W~1             0.009      0.010    0.859    0.391
#>     W~X             0.013      0.013    1.052    0.293
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>     X               1.000                             
#>     Z               1.000                             
#>    .Y               0.467      0.028   16.845    0.000
#>    .W               0.453      0.028   16.068    0.000
#>    .x1              0.140      0.018    7.588    0.000
#>    .x2              0.317      0.019   16.734    0.000
#>    .x3              0.228      0.011   20.324    0.000
#>    .z1              0.161      0.020    8.166    0.000
#>    .z2              0.306      0.019   16.500    0.000
#>    .z3              0.241      0.016   14.867    0.000
#>    .y1              0.162      0.021    7.728    0.000
#>    .y2              0.274      0.019   14.807    0.000
#>    .y3              0.250      0.024   10.484    0.000
#>    .w1              0.160      0.020    8.055    0.000
#>    .w2              0.305      0.019   16.301    0.000
#>    .w3              0.229      0.022   10.490    0.000
#>     Y~1             0.079      0.018    4.394    0.000
#>     Y~X             0.018      0.005    3.728    0.000
#>     Y~Z             0.105      0.016    6.521    0.000
#>     W~1             0.054      0.014    3.968    0.000
#>     W~X             0.095      0.017    5.540    0.000
#>     W~Z             0.144      0.032    4.572    0.000

Ordered Indicators 

fit_slopes_ord <- pls(
  slopes_model,
  data      = randomSlopesOrdered,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50,
  ordered   = colnames(randomSlopesOrdered) # explicitly specify variables as ordered
)
summary(fit_slopes_ord)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 3 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                OrdPLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          PROBIT
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                          5000
#>   Number of iterations                               3
#>   Number of latent variables                         4
#>   Number of observed variables                      18
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                   263.372
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                50
#>   SRMR                                           0.010
#>   RMSEA                                          0.029
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   x1                                             0.870
#>   x2                                             0.669
#>   x3                                             0.789
#>   z1                                             0.841
#>   z2                                             0.714
#>   z3                                             0.758
#>   y1                                             0.844
#>   y2                                             0.711
#>   y3                                             0.754
#>   w1                                             0.835
#>   w2                                             0.681
#>   w3                                             0.785
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   Y                                              0.531
#>   W                                              0.544
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X =~          
#>     x1              0.933      0.015   63.753    0.000
#>     x2              0.818      0.012   71.087    0.000
#>     x3              0.888      0.011   79.818    0.000
#>   Z =~          
#>     z1              0.917      0.011   82.416    0.000
#>     z2              0.845      0.012   68.786    0.000
#>     z3              0.871      0.011   81.612    0.000
#>   Y =~          
#>     y1              0.918      0.010   91.329    0.000
#>     y2              0.843      0.013   63.158    0.000
#>     y3              0.869      0.015   59.199    0.000
#>   W =~          
#>     w1              0.914      0.008  107.793    0.000
#>     w2              0.825      0.017   49.941    0.000
#>     w3              0.886      0.013   66.667    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   Y ~           
#>     X               0.290      0.024   12.132    0.000
#>     Z               0.451      0.035   12.804    0.000
#>   W ~           
#>     X               0.391      0.032   12.187    0.000
#>     Z               0.245      0.047    5.204    0.000
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .Y               0.011      0.005    2.338    0.019
#>    .W               0.009      0.007    1.202    0.229
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   X ~~          
#>     Z               0.168      0.015   11.406    0.000
#>   Y~X ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.006      0.005   -1.285    0.199
#>   Y~Z ~~        
#>     Y~1            -0.024      0.014   -1.717    0.086
#>     Y~X             0.012      0.008    1.508    0.131
#>   W~X ~~        
#>     W~1             0.004      0.009    0.404    0.686
#>   W~Z ~~        
#>     W~1             0.009      0.014    0.666    0.506
#>     W~X             0.016      0.012    1.399    0.162
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>     X               1.000                             
#>     Z               1.000                             
#>    .Y               0.469      0.032   14.724    0.000
#>    .W               0.456      0.026   17.305    0.000
#>    .x1              0.130      0.027    4.797    0.000
#>    .x2              0.331      0.019   17.597    0.000
#>    .x3              0.211      0.020   10.680    0.000
#>    .z1              0.159      0.020    7.806    0.000
#>    .z2              0.286      0.021   13.743    0.000
#>    .z3              0.242      0.019   13.005    0.000
#>    .y1              0.156      0.018    8.498    0.000
#>    .y2              0.289      0.022   12.917    0.000
#>    .y3              0.246      0.025    9.650    0.000
#>    .w1              0.165      0.016   10.646    0.000
#>    .w2              0.319      0.027   11.782    0.000
#>    .w3              0.215      0.024    9.161    0.000
#>     Y~1             0.076      0.019    4.115    0.000
#>     Y~X             0.018      0.005    3.463    0.001
#>     Y~Z             0.101      0.013    7.594    0.000
#>     W~1             0.052      0.011    4.689    0.000
#>     W~X             0.099      0.016    6.316    0.000
#>     W~Z             0.142      0.028    5.122    0.000

Random Intercepts Model 

intercepts_model <- '
  f =~ y1 + y2 + y3
  f ~ x1 + x2 + x3 + w1 + w2 + (1 | cluster)
'

Continuous Indicators 

fit_intercepts_cont <- pls(
  intercepts_model,
  data      = randomIntercepts,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50
)
summary(fit_intercepts_cont)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 2 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                   PLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          LINEAR
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                         10000
#>   Number of iterations                               2
#>   Number of latent variables                         1
#>   Number of observed variables                       9
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                    24.372
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                20
#>   SRMR                                           0.003
#>   RMSEA                                          0.005
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   y1                                             0.891
#>   y2                                             0.785
#>   y3                                             0.814
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   f                                              0.705
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f =~          
#>     y1              0.944      0.007  131.417    0.000
#>     y2              0.886      0.009   94.311    0.000
#>     y3              0.902      0.010   92.275    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f ~           
#>     x1              0.238      0.007   35.744    0.000
#>     x2              0.162      0.008   19.760    0.000
#>     x3              0.077      0.007   10.921    0.000
#>     w1              0.128      0.036    3.544    0.000
#>     w2              0.091      0.035    2.583    0.010
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f              -0.012      0.008   -1.441    0.150
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   x1 ~~         
#>     x2              0.104      0.011    9.746    0.000
#>     x3              0.004      0.013    0.335    0.737
#>     w1              0.000                             
#>     w2              0.000                             
#>   x2 ~~         
#>     x3              0.097      0.011    8.636    0.000
#>     w1              0.000                             
#>     w2              0.000                             
#>   x3 ~~         
#>     w1              0.000                             
#>     w2              0.000                             
#>   w1 ~~         
#>     w2             -0.041      0.059   -0.695    0.487
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f               0.295      0.015   20.333    0.000
#>     x1              1.000                             
#>     x2              1.000                             
#>     x3              1.000                             
#>     w1              1.000                             
#>     w2              1.000                             
#>    .y1              0.109      0.014    8.004    0.000
#>    .y2              0.215      0.017   12.854    0.000
#>    .y3              0.186      0.018   10.603    0.000
#>     f~1             0.582      0.019   30.214    0.000

Ordered Indicators 

fit_intercepts_ord <- pls(
  intercepts_model,
  data      = randomInterceptsOrdered,
  bootstrap = TRUE,
  boot.R    = 50,
  ordered   = colnames(randomInterceptsOrdered) # explicitly specify variables as ordered
)
summary(fit_intercepts_ord)
#> plssem (0.1.1) ended normally after 2 iterations
#> 
#>   Estimator                                OrdPLSc-MLM
#>   Link                                          PROBIT
#>                                                       
#>   Number of observations                         10000
#>   Number of iterations                               2
#>   Number of latent variables                         1
#>   Number of observed variables                       9
#> 
#> Fit Measures:
#>   Chi-Square                                    25.508
#>   Degrees of Freedom                                20
#>   SRMR                                           0.003
#>   RMSEA                                          0.005
#> 
#> R-squared (indicators):
#>   y1                                             0.885
#>   y2                                             0.788
#>   y3                                             0.809
#> 
#> R-squared (latents):
#>   f                                              0.684
#> 
#> Latent Variables:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f =~          
#>     y1              0.941      0.011   85.504    0.000
#>     y2              0.888      0.013   66.758    0.000
#>     y3              0.899      0.012   76.436    0.000
#> 
#> Regressions:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   f ~           
#>     x1              0.241      0.008   28.363    0.000
#>     x2              0.158      0.008   20.875    0.000
#>     x3              0.080      0.007   12.303    0.000
#>     w1              0.121      0.038    3.206    0.001
#>     w2              0.081      0.048    1.683    0.092
#> 
#> Intercepts:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f              -0.011      0.008   -1.476    0.140
#> 
#> Covariances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>   x1 ~~         
#>     x2              0.110      0.012    8.864    0.000
#>     x3              0.012      0.013    0.912    0.362
#>     w1              0.001      0.003    0.240    0.810
#>     w2              0.001      0.003    0.379    0.704
#>   x2 ~~         
#>     x3              0.099      0.012    8.197    0.000
#>     w1             -0.003      0.002   -1.244    0.214
#>     w2             -0.001      0.003   -0.477    0.634
#>   x3 ~~         
#>     w1             -0.003      0.003   -0.933    0.351
#>     w2              0.003      0.003    1.195    0.232
#>   w1 ~~         
#>     w2             -0.025      0.057   -0.435    0.663
#> 
#> Variances:
#>                  Estimate  Std.Error  z.value  P(>|z|)
#>    .f               0.316      0.017   18.131    0.000
#>     x1              1.000                             
#>     x2              1.000                             
#>     x3              1.000                             
#>     w1              1.000                             
#>     w2              1.000                             
#>    .y1              0.115      0.021    5.537    0.000
#>    .y2              0.212      0.024    8.966    0.000
#>    .y3              0.191      0.021    9.030    0.000
#>     f~1             0.562      0.021   27.100    0.000
